preamble: The transformation from the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] to the glottal fricative [h] is a case of debuccalization. This weakening often occurs as consonants lose their specific point of articulation in favor of a glottal state, frequently seen in dialectal evolution.

examples.note: Occurs in several language families as a stage of consonant erosion.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
